revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image for a game-development pixel-art asset.

Canvas and sheet contract:
- Exact final image size: 64x64 pixels.
- 4 rows x 4 columns.
- Each frame/cell is exactly 16x16 pixels.
- 16 frames total.
- Fully opaque PNG, no alpha.
- No visible grid lines, borders, tile IDs, labels, UI, text, watermark, or signatures.
- Every tile fills its 16x16 cell edge to edge and remains contained inside that cell.
- Consistent identity, scale, palette, and top-left light source across all frames.
- Crisp low-resolution 16-bit SNES-style pixel art, sharp pixels, no antialiasing blur, no soft modern brush blur.
- Use painterly pixel clusters and subtle dithering/ramps to imply warm golden-hour light.

Important correction to any conflicting source text:
- The final sheet is 64x64 px with 16x16 px cells, NOT 128x128 px cells.
- This is a top-down terraced rice paddy autotile set, not an ocean coastline set.

Style and theme:
- Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette, cheerful warm golden-hour sunlight.
- Hand-painted Ghibli-like mood translated into crisp SNES-era pixel art.
- World-map material scale: large geographic paddy regions, not close-up village ground detail.
- Terrain tiles should blend naturally and avoid hard rectangular bands or repeated square stamps.
- Organic curved borders throughout, no straight 90-degree corners.

Main material: planted flooded rice paddy, not open blue water.
- Shallow muddy green-brown flooded water.
- The dominant read must be GREEN PLANTED PADDY over muddy water.
- Almost the full water surface is covered by bold, evenly spaced, clearly visible parallel rows of vivid fresh-green young rice seedlings.
- Rice rows must remain unmistakable even when the sheet is shrunk to 1/4 size.
- High contrast: bright green rice clumps with narrow darker muddy-water strips between rows.
- Same row spacing and same row direction across all open-water interior tiles so rows continue seamlessly across neighboring tiles.
- Only thin ribbons of warm-gold reflective water show between seedling rows.
- Add a few tiny specular glints from upper-left light in water gaps.
- Avoid flat blue rectangles, sky-blue mirror water, and broad empty water.

Aze ridge material:
- Low earthen levee ridges of moist dark mud topped by a bright green grass lip.
- Every edge tile uses this 3-point height formula on the ridge side:
  1. bright grass lip at the top/outer crest,
  2. vertical dark mud-and-moss face in the middle,
  3. soft contact shadow at the base where ridge meets paddy water.
- Grass should invade the water margin by 1 pixel where possible at 16px scale.
- Ridge lines must be irregularly curved, gently wavy, and organic, not dead-straight.
- Organic rounded corners, not sharp 90-degree corners.

Strict 4x4 layout, left to right by row:

Row 1: open planted paddy interior variations, no border.
Cell 1: open interior variation A, dense parallel bright-green rice rows over muddy green-brown reflective water.
Cell 2: open interior variation B, same row direction and spacing, minor ripple and glint variation only.
Cell 3: open interior variation C, same seamless rice-row rhythm, slightly different muddy-water highlights.
Cell 4: open interior variation D, same seamless rice-row rhythm, tiny warm-gold reflective gaps.

Row 2: water-to-aze edge tiles in order N, S, W, E.
Cell 5: north edge: organic wavy aze ridge along the north side, water/rice paddy filling the rest, curved grass lip and mud face, shadow below ridge.
Cell 6: south edge: organic wavy aze ridge along the south side, water/rice paddy filling the rest, curved grass lip and mud face, shadow above ridge.
Cell 7: west edge: organic wavy aze ridge along the west side, water/rice paddy filling the rest, curved grass lip and mud face, shadow to the right.
Cell 8: east edge: organic wavy aze ridge along the east side, water/rice paddy filling the rest, curved grass lip and mud face, shadow to the left.

Row 3: outer corner tiles in order NW, NE, SW, SE.
Cell 9: northwest outer corner: north and west aze ridges meet in an organically rounded corner, paddy water/rice in southeast area, grass tufts and mud protrusions soften the curve.
Cell 10: northeast outer corner: north and east aze ridges meet in an organically rounded corner, paddy water/rice in southwest area, reed tips and mud protrusions.
Cell 11: southwest outer corner: south and west aze ridges meet in an organically rounded corner, paddy water/rice in northeast area, grass tufts.
Cell 12: southeast outer corner: south and east aze ridges meet in an organically rounded corner, paddy water/rice in northwest area, mud protrusions and grass lip.

Row 4: accent tiles.
Cell 13: large grass-and-mud peninsula intruding deep into planted paddy water from one side, roughly one-quarter to one-third of the 16px tile depth, organic curved outline, rice rows continue around it.
Cell 14: dense reed cluster at paddy edge, readable reeds with green/yellow tips, organic mud base, planted rice water nearby.
Cell 15: small muddy bald patch on the aze ridge with exposed dark soil and a tiny trickle watermark / water overflow outlet, still warm cheerful palette.
Cell 16: lotus leaf and small frog on still planted paddy water; keep frog tiny but readable, no bird or companion creature.

Tilemap quality:
- Avoid making every tile a repeated square stamp.
- Vary edge breakup, lighting, tufts, mud protrusions, reed tips, and glints while keeping the set cohesive.
- Transition tiles must blend grass/mud/water naturally.
- Avoid hard rectangular bands.
- Avoid straight vertical or horizontal strips of water next to land; all water-to-ridge boundaries should curve or wobble organically.
- No hard pixel staircase edges; use intentional pixel clusters to make curves read smooth while preserving crisp pixel art.

Output visual quality:
- Crisp high-resolution rendering of pixel art at exactly 64x64 px.
- Dark one-pixel outline where useful on ridge faces, reeds, lotus/frog, and mud details.
- Bright cheerful colors, warm gold highlights, earthy greens and browns.
- Solid #FF00FF magenta should fill only any truly empty background space if any exists, but because these are fully filled terrain tiles, no empty space should be visible.

Avoid all of the following:
- No Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos, likenesses, references, emblems, or motifs.
- No samurai armor, kabuto helmet, ninja outfit, two-sword daisho, gun, or modern objects.
- No horror mood, no dark gloomy palette.
- No bird, no companion creature.
- No letter emblems.
- No modern text, watermark, UI, labels, borders, or visible grid.
